papers.title_en: Synthetic APTs: the Collapse of TTP-Based Attribution
papers.authors: Francesco Balassone, Víctor Mayoral-Vilches, María Sanz-Gómez, Paul Zabalegui-Landa, Stefan Rass, Davide Quarta, Daniel Sanchez-Prieto, Marina Oteiza-Álvarez, Almerindo Graziano, Lauren Min Kim, MinSeok Choi
papers.evidence: 使用LLM智能体的多智能体APT网络攻击仿真，直接模拟网络犯罪活动
papers.tldr: 在人工智能时代，基于战术、技术和程序的网络威胁归因面临挑战。研究者部署网络安全超智能框架的AI代理，模仿五个高级持续性威胁组织，攻击由AI防御者守护的企业和军事网络靶场。实验显示，所有企业靶场均遭侵入，攻击者独立将防御方端点管理平台武器化为命令控制通道，行为趋同且未预定义；军事靶场则均成功防御。这表明AI使攻击门槛崩塌，个体可复现国家级APT行为，从而瓦解TTP归因的信度。
papers.abstract_en: Cyber Threat Intelligence CTI attribution relies on identifying the Tactics, Techniques, and Procedures TTPs that distinguish one threat actor from another. This approach presupposes that each adversary leaves a recognizable operational fingerprint. This work investigates whether AI driven adversary emulation challenges that presupposition. We deploy agents from our Cybersecurity SuperIntelligence CSI framework, configured as five Advanced Persistent Threat APT groups, APT28, APT29, APT41, APT44, and Lazarus Group, against AI driven Defender agents across two cyber ranges provided by CYBER RANGES, equipped with defensive software Wazuh, Velociraptor, Elasticsearch and active AI driven defenders: an enterprise network and a military infrastructure. Across 20 experiments using two defender models, a binary pattern emerges: all 10 Enterprise range experiments resulted in compromise 2 to 12 hosts per experiment, while all 10 Military range experiments were successfully defended or resulted in stalemates, regardless of APT profile or defender model. In 8 of 10 Enterprise experiments, attackers independently weaponized the defender's own Velociraptor endpoint management platform as a command and control channel, a convergent behavior not encoded in any threat intelligence profile. We argue that in the AI era, wherein agents can be deployed provided the right models are available and subject to the right scaffolding and agentic configuration, the entry barrier for operating like a nation state APT collapses: beyond nation states, individuals can now act like commonly identified threat actors, and with it, fundamentally undermine TTP based attribution.

papers.title_en: Beyond tokens: a unified framework for latent communication in LLM-based multi-agent systems
papers.evidence: 提供一种可用于网络犯罪模拟的多智能体LLM通信方法
papers.tldr: 多智能体系统通常依靠自然语言逐词元通信，虽解释性强，但造成推理开销大、离散化损失不可逆及语言冗余歧义。本文提出一个统一框架，从通信内容(What)、对齐方式(Which)和融合机制(How)三个维度系统梳理潜在通信方法。我们分类了2024-2026年间的18种代表性方法，归纳出五种设计范式。该框架为领域提供了共同语汇，降低研究门槛，并揭示了跨架构对齐、安全等开放问题。
papers.abstract_en: Multi-agent systems built on large language models (LLMs) have become a prevailing paradigm for tackling complex reasoning, planning, and tool-use tasks. The dominant communication protocol in such systems is natural language: agents exchange messages token-by-token, verbalising their internal reasoning so that peers can read, verify, and respond. While convenient and interpretable, this protocol suffers from three structural drawbacks -- high inference cost, irreversible information loss during discretization, and ambiguity/redundancy of natural language. A growing body of work therefore explores an alternative protocol -- latent communication -- in which agents exchange continuous representations (embeddings, hidden states, or KV-caches) directly, bypassing the bottleneck of text generation. This paper presents a unified framework for organising the rapidly expanding literature on latent communication. We analyse existing methods along three orthogonal axes: (1) WHAT information is communicated (Embeddings, Hidden States, KV-Caches, or other continuous state); (2) WHICH sender-receiver alignment is used (latent-space alignment and layer alignment); and (3) HOW the communicated information is fused into the receiver (concatenation, prepending, mathematical operations, cross-attention, or cache restoration). Under this 3-axis framework, we systematically categorise eighteen representative methods proposed between 2024 and 2026, identify five major design patterns, and surface a set of open challenges -- including cross-architecture alignment, security of latent channels, compression for edge deployment, and the relationship between latent communication and latent chain-of-thought. We hope that this framework both lowers the barrier to entry for new researchers and provides a vocabulary for comparing future work.

papers.title_en: Adversarial Creation and Detection of AI-Generated Social Bot Content
papers.authors: Mykola Trokhymovych, Ricardo Baeza-Yates, Alessandro Flammini, Diego Saez-Trumper, Filippo Menczer
papers.evidence: 利用对抗方法学模拟恶意行为者使用LLM进行社交机器人冒充，展示LLM在网络犯罪内容生成中的能力
papers.tldr: 大型语言模型驱动的社交机器人可规模化生成类人内容，但现有AI内容检测因缺乏真实对抗数据而失效。本文通过模拟恶意用户冒充行为，构建多语言、跨平台的人机消息对数据集，并基于此训练检测模型。在分布外真实数据上，该方法显著优于现有内容检测方案，有效提升了检测鲁棒性。该研究为防御社交机器人操纵信息生态提供了新思路。
papers.abstract_en: The convergence of large language models and social bots allows malicious actors to manipulate the information ecosystem by generating human-like content at scale. Existing models for detecting AI-generated content often fail in the wild, primarily due to the lack of ground-truth data. We address this gap through an adversarial methodology that models the impersonation of real social media users by malicious actors. Using this methodology, we curate a multilingual, cross-platform dataset of paired human and AI-generated messages. Training on such adversarial data yields accurate detection of AI-generated text. Our approach significantly outperforms existing models for content-based bot detection in real-world, out-of-distribution data.

papers.title_en: Beyond tokens: a unified framework for latent communication in LLM-based multi-agent systems
papers.evidence: 提出基于大语言模型的多智能体系统潜通信方法，可提升模拟网络犯罪活动的效率。
papers.tldr: 当前LLM多智能体系统普遍采用自然语言通信，面临推理成本高、离散化信息损失与表达冗余等瓶颈。本文提出一个统一框架，从通信内容、发送-接收对齐和融合方式三个正交维度系统梳理潜在连续通信方法。该框架对2024至2026年提出的18种方法进行分类，归纳出五种核心设计模式，并指出跨架构对齐、安全性等开放挑战。该工作为潜在通信领域提供结构化词汇，降低研究门槛，促进方法比较与创新。
papers.abstract_en: Multi-agent systems built on large language models (LLMs) have become a prevailing paradigm for tackling complex reasoning, planning, and tool-use tasks. The dominant communication protocol in such systems is natural language: agents exchange messages token-by-token, verbalising their internal reasoning so that peers can read, verify, and respond. While convenient and interpretable, this protocol suffers from three structural drawbacks -- high inference cost, irreversible information loss during discretization, and ambiguity/redundancy of natural language. A growing body of work therefore explores an alternative protocol -- latent communication -- in which agents exchange continuous representations (embeddings, hidden states, or KV-caches) directly, bypassing the bottleneck of text generation. This paper presents a unified framework for organising the rapidly expanding literature on latent communication. We analyse existing methods along three orthogonal axes: (1) WHAT information is communicated (Embeddings, Hidden States, KV-Caches, or other continuous state); (2) WHICH sender-receiver alignment is used (latent-space alignment and layer alignment); and (3) HOW the communicated information is fused into the receiver (concatenation, prepending, mathematical operations, cross-attention, or cache restoration). Under this 3-axis framework, we systematically categorise eighteen representative methods proposed between 2024 and 2026, identify five major design patterns, and surface a set of open challenges -- including cross-architecture alignment, security of latent channels, compression for edge deployment, and the relationship between latent communication and latent chain-of-thought. We hope that this framework both lowers the barrier to entry for new researchers and provides a vocabulary for comparing future work.

papers.title_en: TRACE: Trajectory Reasoning through Adaptive Cross-Step Evidence Aggregation for LLM Agents
papers.authors: Vijitha Mittapalli, Shreyaa Jayant Dani, Satya Srujana Pilli, Snigdha Ansu, Mohammadreza Teymoorianfard, Franck Dernoncourt, Hongjie Chen, Yu Wang, Ryan A. Rossi, Nesreen K. Ahmed
papers.evidence: 检测大语言模型智能体恶意目标的监测框架，支持网络背景下的对抗测试。
papers.tldr: LLM代理可在长序列任务中执行表面合法但暗藏恶意的操作，传统监控因只能整体评估或分窗独立评分，无法关联远距离动作间的隐蔽证据。为应对此挑战，本文提出TRACE框架，其核心是TIJ循环，通过分诊定位高信号片段，在检查过程中保持并累积跨步证据，最终形成轨迹级判决。在十个SHADE-Arena任务域上，TRACE达到了0.713的F1分数和0.844的召回率，长程证据连接任务提升最为显著。该工作为长时域代理的恶意行为监测提供了有效技术途径。
papers.abstract_en: Autonomous LLM agents can pursue hidden malicious objectives through sequences of individually benign actions, making sabotage difficult to detect using standard trajectory-level monitoring. Existing approaches either evaluate complete trajectories in a single pass or partition them into independently scored windows, limiting their ability to connect evidence across temporally distant actions. We propose TRACE, a monitoring framework for long-horizon LLM agent trajectories. TRACE operates through a TIJ (Triage-Inspect-Judge) loop that identifies high-signal regions, performs targeted inspection while maintaining accumulated evidence across reasoning steps, and synthesizes a trajectory-level verdict. We evaluate TRACE on ten task domains from SHADE-Arena against state-of-the-art baselines. TRACE achieves an aggregate F1 of 0.713 and recall of 0.844, with the largest gains on tasks requiring long-range evidence linking.
